skill.name: playwright-best-practices
skill.description: Guides AI coding agents to write reliable Playwright tests with resilient locators, fixtures, page objects, web-first assertions, authentication setup, and network mocking.
skill.summary: Apply proven Playwright testing patterns for stable end-to-end tests.
skill.author: 0xBigBoss

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, environment, and weak-crypto patterns, but review shows they are Markdown examples for Playwright tests. No prompt injection, hidden execution path, third-party exfiltration, or malicious intent was found. The only residual concern is low-risk guidance around test credentials and saved auth state, which is normal when kept gitignored and scoped to test accounts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The repeated external command findings come from Markdown code fences and inline backticks that document Playwright CLI usage. They do not define an executable script, dynamic command construction, or automatic command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is contained in Markdown examples for npx Playwright commands and reporter flags. No executable file or user-controlled shell interpolation exists in the skill package.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Relative Imports Flagged as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The path traversal findings are TypeScript import examples that reference page objects with normal relative paths. They are not filesystem reads and are not influenced by user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged '../pages' strings are static import paths inside a documentation code block. There is no file access API, path concatenation, or runtime traversal behavior.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Test Credential Examples Require Care
low_findings.description: Verdict: NEEDS_REVIEW. The skill shows Playwright patterns that read test credentials from environment variables and save browser auth state under playwright/.auth. This is acceptable for test automation, but users must use test accounts and keep auth state files out of version control.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credential handling is clearly part of standard Playwright authentication setup, and the project structure labels .auth as gitignored. It remains a real operational caution because saved storage state can contain session material.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Local Test Network Calls
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The network findings are Playwright request and route examples against the application under test or localhost. No evidence found of a third-party endpoint, covert beacon, or credential exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples use relative API paths, route.fetch for mocked responses, and localhost configuration. These are expected Playwright testing patterns and do not send data to an external service.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Heuristic
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography findings appear to be triggered by frontmatter text, object model terms, browser device names, and crypto.randomUUID in a test data factory example. No weak hashing or encryption algorithm is recommended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain descriptive text, Playwright browser device names, and crypto.randomUUID for test identifiers. I did not find evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic use.

content.user_title: Improve Playwright Tests With Stable Patterns
content.value_statement: Flaky end-to-end tests waste engineering time and make releases harder to trust. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward durable Playwright patterns for locators, fixtures, assertions, auth, and mocks.
actual_capabilities: Recommends resilient Playwright locators such as roles, labels, and test IDs.
Shows page object and fixture patterns for reusable test setup.
Explains web-first assertions that reduce timing-related flakiness.
Provides authentication setup patterns using storage state.
Covers network mocking, route handling, and HAR-based response replay.
Suggests minimal CLI reporters for agent and CI test runs.
limitations: Does not execute tests or inspect a repository by itself.
Does not replace project-specific accessibility and test data design.
Requires Playwright and @playwright/test knowledge from the surrounding agent.
Authentication examples must be adapted for each application's security model.

use_cases.title: Refactor Flaky Tests
use_cases.description: Convert brittle selectors, fixed waits, and manual assertions into stable Playwright patterns.

use_cases.title: Create Test Architecture
use_cases.description: Design fixtures, page objects, helpers, and setup projects for a growing end-to-end suite.

use_cases.title: Optimize Agent Test Runs
use_cases.description: Use concise reporters and focused debugging commands when an AI agent runs Playwright tests.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Locator
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Playwright test and replace brittle CSS or XPath locators with resilient user-facing locators.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Test Flakiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this Playwright spec to remove hardcoded waits and use web-first assertions with clear failure messages.

prompt_templates.title: Add Fixtures and Page Objects
prompt_templates.prompt: Design reusable Playwright fixtures and page objects for this user journey while keeping tests isolated.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Authenticated Test Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Playwright authentication setup that reuses storage state, avoids repeated UI login, and protects test credentials.

output_examples.input: A test clicks a button using a long CSS class selector and waits for two seconds.
output_examples.output: The skill would suggest a role-based locator, an awaited visibility or enabled assertion, and removal of the fixed wait.

output_examples.input: A suite logs in through the UI before every test.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend a setup project that authenticates once and reuses storage state for dependent browser projects.

output_examples.input: A test needs stable API responses without hitting a real backend.
output_examples.output: The skill would suggest setting up routes before navigation and fulfilling requests with controlled fixture data.

best_practices: Prefer role, label, placeholder, text, alt text, title, and test ID locators before CSS or XPath.
Use awaited Playwright assertions so tests retry until the expected browser state appears.
Keep auth state files gitignored and use scoped test accounts for automated login.
anti_patterns: Do not use fixed timeouts when Playwright can wait for a visible or enabled element.
Do not share mutable state between tests that must be isolated.
Do not run verbose default reporters in constrained agent or CI contexts.

faq.question: Does this skill run Playwright tests?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for an AI coding agent that writes or modifies Playwright tests.

faq.question: Which Playwright language does it target?
Can it help reduce flaky tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes resilient locators, web-first assertions, isolated tests, and avoiding hardcoded waits.

faq.question: Does it support authentication flows?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents setup projects and storage state patterns for reusing authenticated sessions.

faq.question: Is saved auth state safe?
faq.answer: It can be safe for test accounts when the file is gitignored and protected from logs or artifacts.

faq.question: Can it help with network mocking?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers route mocking, modifying responses, and replaying recorded responses from HAR files.
